relations.note: The restriction on gold and sunshine text on white backgrounds is a specific application of the general WCAG contrast rules.

quotes: Test contrast levels (webaim.com or equivalent) for every text/background pair.
[GENERAL minimums: WCAG AA — 4.5:1 body text, 3:1 large text ≥24px or 19px bold.]

rules.intent: Ensure readability and accessibility compliance across all digital assets.
rules.rule_text: Contrast levels must satisfy WCAG AA minimums (4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text >= 24px or 19px bold). Every text and background pair must be tested to ensure these compliance baselines are met.

quotes: Avoid: Gold or Sunshine text on White (fails contrast) — golds are for surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis only.

rules.intent: Prevent unreadable text treatments and enforce WCAG contrast limits.
rules.rule_text: Do not use Gold (color.primary.gold = #ffc60a) or Sunshine (color.primary.sunshine = #faa31b) text on White backgrounds as it fails accessibility contrast limits. Gold and yellow values are strictly reserved for surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis.

quotes: Don't rely on color alone to convey meaning — pair with icons, depth, clear copy, or visual feedback.

rules.intent: Maintain usability for colorblind and visually impaired users.
rules.rule_text: Do not rely solely on color to communicate informational meaning, state changes, or feedback; always pair color shifts with supporting indicators such as text labels, icons, depth, or clear structural copy.
